intro: Iria vaginata, also known as Iria, is a plant species within the Iridaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of iris. The Iria plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to lower blood pressure, alleviate inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, contributing to overall cardiovascular health and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Iria vaginata include iridoids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic properties. Iria is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicine formulas to promote cardiovascular well-being and immune function.
